content.problemStatement:
The mayor of Townsville City wants to build a telephone network in the city.

The city is represented as a square grid of size N√óNN \times NN√óN. Rows and columns of the grid are numbered from 111 to NNN. Each cell of the grid (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) has a tower of height Hi,jH_{i, j}Hi,j‚Äč. The height of each tower is between 111 and KKK (inclusive). It is guaranteed that there is at least one tower of each height in the city.


The mayor's engineering team wants to use exactly K‚ąí1K - 1K‚ąí1 cables to create the network.
The first cable should be between a tower of height 111 and a tower of height 222, the second cable should be between a tower of height 222 and a tower of height 333, and so on with the (K‚ąí1)th(K - 1)^{th}(K‚ąí1)th cable connecting a tower of height (K‚ąí1)(K - 1)(K‚ąí1) and a tower of height KKK, i.e., a chain of cables needs to be created in the height order 1,2,3,‚Ä¶,K1, 2, 3, \dots, K1,2,3,‚Ä¶,K.
The cost of connecting two cells (i1,j1)(i_1, j_1)(i1‚Äč,j1‚Äč) and (i2,j2)(i_2, j_2)(i2‚Äč,j2‚Äč) is ‚ą£i2‚ąíi1‚ą£+‚ą£j2‚ąíj1‚ą£|i_2 - i_1| + |j_2 - j_1|‚ą£i2‚Äč‚ąíi1‚Äč‚ą£+‚ą£j2‚Äč‚ąíj1‚Äč‚ą£.
For all iii such that 1‚Č§i<K1 \leq i < K1‚Č§i<K, the ithi^{th}ith and (i+1)th(i+1)^{th}(i+1)th cables should have one tower in common.
Find the minimum total cost of the chain of cables required to build the telephone network.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains 222 space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
NNN lines follow. Each line contains NNN space-separated integers. The jthj^{th}jth number in the ithi^{th}ith line represents Hi,jH_{i, j}Hi,j‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the minimum cost of cables used.
constraints: 1‚Č§Hi,j‚Č§K1 \le H_{i, j} \le K1‚Č§Hi,j‚Äč‚Č§K for each valid (i,j)(i, j)(i,j)
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 100100100.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1:

One of the chains that can be formed with minimum cost is (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) -> (2,1)(2, 1)(2,1) -> (3,1)(3, 1)(3,1) with cost 222.
Even though (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) -> (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) -> (3,1)(3, 1)(3,1) is also a valid chain (among other valid chains), its cost is 555 which is not the minimum.
